subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the concept of habitat and its components
Differentiate between biotic and abiotic components
Understand population and community in ecosystems
Identify interactions between organisms and their environment
Explain food chains and food webs
Understand the role of producers, consumers, and decomposers
Recognize the importance of ecosystem balance and conservation

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Habitat
topics_covered: Examples of habitats like ponds and forests
Importance of suitable conditions for organisms
teaching_strategy: Use pictures of forest and pond ecosystems
Ask students about animals they see around them
student_activities: List living and non-living things in a nearby habitat
Discuss why certain animals live in specific habitats

lesson_plan.title: Biotic and Abiotic Components
topics_covered: Interactions between living and non-living components
student_activities: Create a table listing biotic and abiotic components
Identify these components in a school garden

lesson_plan.title: Population and Community
student_activities: Observe organisms in a garden area
Count individuals of one plant species to identify population

lesson_plan.title: Ecosystem and Interactions
topics_covered: Interaction between biotic and abiotic components
teaching_strategy: Examples from forests, rivers, and farms
student_activities: Identify interactions between organisms and environment

lesson_plan.title: Food Chain and Food Web
topics_covered: Concept of producers, consumers, and decomposers
teaching_strategy: Step-by-step drawing of a food chain
student_activities: Draw a food chain using grass, grasshopper, frog, snake, eagle
Create a simple food web diagram

lesson_plan.title: Decomposition and Ecosystem Balance
teaching_strategy: Explain with examples of fungi and bacteria
student_activities: Observe decomposition of leaves in soil

teaching_aids: Charts of food chains and ecosystems
Pictures of forest and pond ecosystems
teacher_tip: Use real-life examples such as gardens, ponds, and forests around the school to help students understand ecosystems and the interactions between organisms.
